terms.definition: Combines the Investigator (Line 1) and Martyr (Line 3). Needs a solid foundation of knowledge and learns through trial and error.
Combines the Investigator (Line 1) and Opportunist (Line 4). Builds a secure knowledge foundation then shares it through their personal network.
The house of career, public reputation, authority, and achievements. The 10th House cusp is the Midheaven (MC), and this house governs professional ambitions, social standing, and one's legacy.

The house of friendships, groups, social networks, and hopes for the future. The 11th House governs community involvement, humanitarian causes, and the broader social circles that support one's aspirations.
The house of the unconscious, hidden matters, solitude, and spiritual transcendence. The 12th House governs isolation, self-undoing, dreams, institutions, and the dissolution of the ego.

An alternative zodiac system that includes Ophiuchus as a 13th sign between Scorpio and Sagittarius, based on the actual constellations the Sun passes through. It uses IAU constellation boundaries, resulting in signs of unequal length. It is not used in mainstream astrology but periodically generates public interest.

The house of self, identity, physical appearance, and first impressions. The 1st House cusp is the Ascendant, making it one of the most significant houses, governing how a person presents themselves to the world.
Combines the Hermit (Line 2) and Opportunist (Line 4). Has natural talents that need to be called out by others through their network.
Combines the Hermit (Line 2) and Heretic (Line 5). Carries a natural gift combined with the 5th line projection field where others project expectations.
The house of personal finances, possessions, values, and self-worth. The 2nd House governs material resources, earning potential, and what a person finds valuable or worthy of investment.
Combines the Martyr (Line 3) and Heretic (Line 5). Learns through trial and error while carrying a projection field others look to for practical solutions.
Combines the Martyr (Line 3) and Role Model (Line 6). Three life phases: intense trial-and-error until about 30, observing from the roof until around 50, then stepping into authenticity.
The house of communication, siblings, short journeys, and the immediate environment. The 3rd House governs how a person thinks, speaks, writes, and interacts with their local community and neighbors.
Combines the Opportunist (Line 4) and Investigator (Line 1). A fixed fate profile with a rigid foundation and strong network orientation.
Combines the Opportunist (Line 4) and Role Model (Line 6). Deeply connected to their network, going through three life phases to develop into authentic role models.

The house of home, family, roots, and emotional foundations. The 4th House cusp is the IC (Imum Coeli), and this house governs private life, ancestry, real estate, and the conditions at the end of life.
Combines the Heretic (Line 5) and Investigator (Line 1). Carries a strong projection field and builds authority on deep investigation and research.
Combines the Heretic (Line 5) and Hermit (Line 2). Carries a projection field while possessing natural talents, called out to provide solutions while needing retreat.
The house of creativity, romance, children, pleasure, and self-expression. The 5th House governs hobbies, love affairs, artistic pursuits, and all forms of joyful, spontaneous activity.
Combines the Role Model (Line 6) and Hermit (Line 2). Moves through three life phases while carrying natural talents that others must call out.
Combines the Role Model (Line 6) and Martyr (Line 3). Intensely experiential, with both lines involving learning through trial and error across the three life phases.
The house of daily routines, health, work, and service. The 6th House governs employment (as opposed to career), physical wellness, habits, and one's relationship to duty and practical tasks.

The house of partnerships, marriage, open enemies, and one-on-one relationships. The 7th House cusp is the Descendant, and this house governs committed relationships, business partnerships, and contracts.
The house of transformation, shared resources, death, and rebirth. The 8th House governs inheritance, taxes, debts, intimacy, psychology, and the deeper mysteries of life and the occult.
The house of higher education, philosophy, long-distance travel, and belief systems. The 9th House governs religion, law, publishing, foreign cultures, and the search for meaning and truth.
BG5 Career Type equivalent of the Projector. Natural guide who excels at directing others' energy when recognized and invited.
One of the four classical elements in astrology, encompassing Gemini, Libra, and Aquarius. Air signs are associated with intellect, communication, social connection, and abstract thinking.
The awareness center for conceptualization and mental processing, located between the Head and Throat Centers. Responsible for how we process information and form opinions.

One of the four Royal Stars of Persia, located at approximately 9 degrees Gemini in tropical astrology. Aldebaran is the eye of the constellation Taurus and is associated with honor, courage, eloquence, and material success, provided integrity is maintained.

A binary star system in the constellation Perseus, located at approximately 26 degrees Taurus in tropical astrology. Algol is traditionally considered one of the most malefic fixed stars, associated with losing one's head (literally or metaphorically), violence, and intense transformation.

The planet with the most essential dignity points at a given degree, calculated by tallying domicile, exaltation, triplicity, term, and face rulerships. The almuten of the chart (almuten figuris) is the planet with the greatest combined dignity across several key chart points.
The channel of leadership within a small group in Penta mechanics, connecting to natural hierarchy and decision-making flow.

The planet with the second-highest degree in the birth chart, serving as the significator of career and professional life in Jaimini astrology. It acts as the advisor or minister to the atmakaraka (soul significator). Its sign and house placement indicate the field through which the native expresses their professional purpose.

The 29th degree (29 degrees 0 minutes through 29 degrees 59 minutes) of any zodiac sign, considered a point of urgency and culmination. Planets at the anaretic degree are finishing their journey through a sign and carry a sense of crisis, completion, or karmic intensity.
The Not-Self theme of the Manifestor Type. When not informing before acting, they encounter resistance that generates anger.

The 1st, 4th, 7th, and 10th houses, which correspond to the four chart angles. Angular houses are considered the most powerful and visible positions in a chart, and planets placed here tend to have a strong, direct influence on a person's life.

A traditional timing technique that advances the Ascendant one whole sign per year of life, activating a new house and its ruling planet each birthday. The activated house lord becomes the Lord of the Year, whose transits and condition carry special significance for that twelve-month period.

The sub-period within a mahadasha in the Vimshottari Dasha system. Each mahadasha is subdivided into antardasha periods ruled by each of the nine grahas in sequence. The antardasha planet modifies and refines the themes of the ruling mahadasha planet.

One of the four Royal Stars of Persia, located at approximately 9 degrees Sagittarius in tropical astrology. Antares is the heart of the constellation Scorpius and is associated with courage, intensity, obsessive drive, and success through bold action, but with a risk of self-destruction.

Mirror points across the Cancer-Capricorn axis (the solstice axis) where two zodiac degrees share the same amount of daylight. Planets in antiscia form a hidden connection, functioning as a covert conjunction, and are used in traditional astrology to reveal secret alliances or unseen influences.

The seventeenth nakshatra in Vedic astrology, spanning 3 degrees 20 minutes to 16 degrees 40 minutes of Scorpio. Its ruling deity is Mitra (god of friendship), and its planetary ruler is Saturn. It is associated with devotion, loyalty, and the ability to maintain deep friendships and alliances.
One of six Determination types (Color 1). The body eats based on hunger signals, with natural appetite guiding when and what to consume.

An aspect where the faster-moving planet is approaching the exact degree of the aspect with the slower-moving planet. Applying aspects are generally considered stronger and more active than separating aspects because the energy is building toward its peak.

The eleventh sign of the zodiac, spanning 300 to 330 degrees of the ecliptic. Aquarius is a fixed air sign traditionally ruled by Saturn and modernly by Uranus, associated with innovation, humanitarianism, and individuality.

Calculated sensitive points derived from the mathematical relationships between three chart factors, typically two planets and an angle. Arabic Parts (or Lots) have origins in Hellenistic astrology and include dozens of variations addressing specific life topics such as fortune, spirit, marriage, and career.

A philosophical approach to astrology developed by Richard Tarnas and others that views planetary alignments as correlating with archetypal patterns in human experience and world events. It draws heavily on the work of C.G. Jung and emphasizes that planets do not cause events but reflect universal archetypal dynamics. Tarnas's book Cosmos and Psyche is the foundational text of this approach.

The sixth nakshatra in Vedic astrology, spanning 6 degrees 40 minutes to 20 degrees of Gemini. Its ruling deity is Rudra (the storm god), and its planetary ruler is Rahu. It is associated with destruction that precedes renewal, effort, and intellectual intensity.
The first sign of the zodiac, spanning 0 to 30 degrees of the ecliptic. Aries is a cardinal fire sign ruled by Mars, associated with initiative, courage, and assertiveness.

One of four directional indicators at the top of the Human Design bodygraph. Each Arrow points either left (focused, strategic) or right (receptive, peripheral). The four Arrows relate to Determination, Environment, Cognition, and Perspective.

The zodiac sign and degree rising on the eastern horizon at the exact time and place of birth. The Ascendant defines the cusp of the 1st House and strongly influences outward personality, physical appearance, and how others perceive you.

The ninth nakshatra in Vedic astrology, spanning 16 degrees 40 minutes to 30 degrees of Cancer. Its ruling deity is the Nagas (serpent deities), and its planetary ruler is Mercury. It is associated with mystical power, clinging, and kundalini energy.

A numerical scoring system in Vedic astrology that evaluates the benefic and malefic contributions of each planet to every sign. Each sign receives points (bindus) from each of the seven planets and the ascendant, producing a score out of a maximum of 8 per planet. The total score (Sarvashtakavarga) for each sign helps determine favorable and unfavorable transits.

The first nakshatra in Vedic astrology, spanning 0 to 13 degrees 20 minutes of Aries. Its ruling deity is the Ashwini Kumaras (divine physicians), and its planetary ruler is Ketu. It is associated with healing, speed, and new beginnings.

An angular relationship between two planets in an astrological chart. Major aspects include Conjunction (0 degrees), Sextile (60), Square (90), Trine (120), and Opposition (180). Aspects reveal how planetary energies interact.

The planet that holds the highest degree in any sign in the birth chart, considered the significator of the soul in Jaimini astrology. It reveals the deepest desires and karmic lessons the soul is working through in this lifetime. The sign and house placement of the atmakaraka in the navamsa (karakamsa) are especially significant.

The body's inner decision-making mechanism in Human Design. Authority tells you how to make correct decisions for yourself. Types of Authority include Emotional, Sacral, Splenic, Ego, Self-Projected, Mental, and Lunar.

The angular difference between the Tropical and Sidereal zodiacs caused by the precession of the equinoxes. Different ayanamsa values (such as Lahiri or Fagan-Bradley) produce slightly different sidereal chart placements. The value increases by approximately one degree every 72 years.
The foundational sub-frequency beneath Tone in the hierarchy of Color, Tone, Base. There are five Bases (1-5) further differentiating each Tone.

A classification for planets considered to produce favorable or supportive outcomes in traditional astrology. Jupiter is the Greater Benefic and Venus is the Lesser Benefic, generally bringing expansion, harmony, and ease to the areas they influence.
The career and business application of Human Design. BG5 analyzes how individuals function within small group dynamics (Penta) and larger organizational structures (WA/OC16).
The business application of Human Design focused on individual career potential and team dynamics. Uses five Career Types.

The second nakshatra in Vedic astrology, spanning 13 degrees 20 minutes to 26 degrees 40 minutes of Aries. Its ruling deity is Yama (god of death and dharma), and its planetary ruler is Venus. It is associated with creation, restraint, and transformation.

The Sanskrit term for an astrological house in Vedic astrology. There are 12 bhavas, each governing specific areas of life such as wealth, relationships, or career. Vedic astrology most commonly uses the Whole Sign house system for bhava calculation.

A minor aspect formed when two planets are approximately 144 degrees apart in the zodiac. Like the quintile, the bi-quintile relates to creative expression and special abilities, often indicating a talent that develops through conscious effort.
One of four animal categories in the Variable system. Carries a specific combination of strategic and receptive orientations influencing cognitive and metabolic design.
The Not-Self theme of the Projector Type. When initiating or seeking attention without waiting for invitation, deep bitterness results.

A mathematical point representing the lunar apogee, or the point in the Moon's orbit farthest from Earth. Black Moon Lilith symbolizes repressed desires, raw feminine power, and the shadow side of the psyche.

The visual map of a person's Human Design, showing nine Centers connected by Channels and Gates. The bodygraph combines elements of the I Ching, Kabbalah Tree of Life, Hindu-Brahmin Chakra system, and quantum physics.

A Hellenistic concept where a benefic planet (Jupiter or Venus) improves the condition of another planet or house through aspect, conjunction, or reception. It is the positive counterpart to maltreatment and is used to assess whether a planet receives helpful support. The concept was central to the Hellenistic framework for evaluating planetary condition.

The 3rd, 6th, 9th, and 12th houses, which precede the angular houses. Cadent houses are associated with learning, adaptation, and mental or spiritual processes, and planets here are traditionally considered less outwardly powerful.

A house system attributed to the 13th-century mathematician Campanus of Novara that divides the prime vertical into equal 30-degree arcs. These arcs are then projected onto the ecliptic to determine house cusps. It emphasizes the local spatial environment of the birthplace.
The fourth sign of the zodiac, spanning 90 to 120 degrees of the ecliptic. Cancer is a cardinal water sign ruled by the Moon, associated with nurturing, emotional sensitivity, and home.
The tenth sign of the zodiac, spanning 270 to 300 degrees of the ecliptic. Capricorn is a cardinal earth sign ruled by Saturn, associated with ambition, discipline, and structure.

One of the three modalities in astrology, encompassing Aries, Cancer, Libra, and Capricorn. Cardinal signs initiate action and mark the beginning of each season, representing leadership and new beginnings.
One of six Environment types (Color 1). An enclosed, selective space where one thrives by being sheltered and choosing carefully who enters.

A planetary condition where a planet is within 17 minutes of arc from the exact center of the Sun. A cazimi planet is considered to be in the heart of the Sun and is traditionally viewed as greatly strengthened, receiving royal favor and exceptional clarity.

One of nine energy hubs in the Human Design bodygraph. Centers can be defined (colored in, consistent energy) or undefined/open (white, taking in and amplifying energy from others). The nine Centers are Head, Ajna, Throat, G/Self, Heart/Ego, Sacral, Solar Plexus, Spleen, and Root.

A dwarf planet and the largest body in the asteroid belt, representing nurturing, sustenance, and the cycles of loss and return in astrology. Ceres indicates how a person gives and receives care, as well as themes of food, agriculture, and mothering.

A connection between two Centers in the bodygraph, created when both Gates on either end are defined. There are 36 Channels in Human Design. A defined Channel creates Definition and brings specific life force energy and themes.

A system of variable planetary significators in Jaimini astrology where each planet is assigned a specific role based on its degree position in the chart. The seven or eight chara karakas include Atmakaraka (soul), Amatyakaraka (career), and others signifying children, spouse, parents, and siblings. Unlike fixed karakas, these change from chart to chart.

The planet that rules the sign on the Ascendant, serving as the primary significator for the native in traditional and modern astrology. The chart ruler's sign, house, and aspects provide key information about a person's overall life direction and temperament.

A small celestial body orbiting between Saturn and Uranus, known as the Wounded Healer in astrology. Chiron's placement in the birth chart indicates deep wounds and the potential for profound healing and teaching through personal experience.
Around age 50-51. Brings encounter with core wounds and potential for profound healing, wisdom, and ability to guide others.

The fourteenth nakshatra in Vedic astrology, spanning 23 degrees 20 minutes of Virgo to 6 degrees 40 minutes of Libra. Its ruling deity is Vishvakarma (divine architect), and its planetary ruler is Mars. It is associated with brilliance, artistry, and the creation of beautiful forms.
A grouping of channels sharing a common theme and energy flow. Three main groups: Individual, Tribal, and Collective, each with a distinct way of operating.
BG5 Career Type equivalent of the Generator. Workforce powerhouse with sustainable life force, designed to respond to the right work.
The perceptual awareness aspect of Variable in Human Design, derived from the top-right Arrow. It indicates how your mind is designed to take in and process information from the world.
Contains the Understanding (Logic) and Sensing (Abstract) Circuits. Theme is sharing, designed to benefit humanity through patterns and experience.
A layer beneath the Line in the substructure of each activation. There are six Colors per Line. Color is the basis for Determination, Environment, Cognition, and Perspective in the Variable system.

A planetary condition where a planet is within approximately 8 degrees of the Sun but outside cazimi range. A combust planet is considered weakened or hidden because the Sun's light overwhelms its visibility and capacity to function.

A single chart created by calculating the midpoints between corresponding planets in two natal charts. The composite chart represents the relationship itself as its own entity, revealing the purpose, dynamics, and potential of the partnership.

A relationship chart created by calculating the midpoint between each pair of corresponding planets and points in two people's charts. The resulting composite chart represents the relationship itself as a distinct entity with its own dynamics. It is typically used alongside synastry to gain a fuller picture of how a partnership functions.
Energy entering through undefined and open centers from others, planets, and the neutrino field. Problematic when identified with and used for decision-making.

A major aspect formed when two planets occupy the same or nearly the same degree of the zodiac, typically within an orb of 0 to 10 degrees. Conjunctions represent a fusion of planetary energies, intensifying and blending their combined influence.

Mirror points across the Aries-Libra axis (the equinox axis), representing the opposite of antiscia. Planets in contra-antiscia form a hidden opposition and can indicate concealed tensions or adversarial relationships that operate beneath the surface.

Specific degrees within the zodiac considered to carry heightened energy and significance. The most commonly cited critical degrees are 0, 13, and 26 degrees of cardinal signs, 8 to 9 and 21 to 22 degrees of fixed signs, and 4 and 17 degrees of mutable signs.

A divisional chart in Vedic astrology created by dividing each sign into ten equal parts of 3 degrees each. It is specifically used to analyze career, professional achievements, and public status. The placement and strength of planets in the dashamsa refine the career indications of the birth chart.

A relationship chart calculated using the midpoint in time and space between two people's births, then casting a real chart for that moment and location. Unlike the composite chart, the Davison chart represents an actual moment in time and can be read like a standard natal chart.

A relationship chart calculated by finding the midpoint in time and space between two people's birth data, producing a single chart for an actual moment in time. Unlike the composite chart, the Davison chart corresponds to a real date, time, and location. It is used to assess the overall character, purpose, and trajectory of a relationship.

A 10-degree subdivision of each zodiac sign, dividing it into three equal parts. Each decan has its own planetary ruler and adds subtle variation to a sign's expression, distinguishing planets at early, middle, and late degrees of the same sign.

A Hellenistic time lord technique that divides life into successive periods ruled by pairs of planets, based on the sect of the chart and the position of the luminaries. Each major period lasts a specific number of years, with sub-periods ruled by a second planet. The system was described by the 2nd-century astrologer Vettius Valens.
The process of releasing conditioning patterns from open and undefined centers. Takes approximately seven years as every cell is replaced. Begins when living by Strategy and Authority.
A center that is colored in with at least one complete channel. Represents consistent, reliable energy that radiates outward to condition others.

How the defined Centers in a bodygraph are connected to each other. Types of Definition include Single (all defined Centers connected), Split (two separate groups), Triple Split (three groups), and Quadruple Split (four groups). No Definition is a Reflector.
A deep sleep state where the Personality Crystal leaves the body entirely, leaving it to the Design Crystal's intelligence. Part of why sleeping alone in one's own aura matters.

The zodiac sign and degree setting on the western horizon at the time of birth, directly opposite the Ascendant. The Descendant defines the cusp of the 7th House and reveals qualities a person seeks in partners and close relationships.

The unconscious side of a Human Design chart, calculated approximately 88 days before birth. Design activations are shown in red on the bodygraph. These represent qualities that operate unconsciously and are often more visible to others.
The body's unconscious intelligence and bio-genetic inheritance. Calculated from approximately 88 degrees of the Sun's arc before birth. Corresponds to the red activations.
One of six Motivation types (Color 3). Drives awareness through wanting and passion, locking onto specific goals.
The dietary regimen aspect of Variable in Human Design, derived from the top-left Arrow. It indicates how you are designed to best take in food and nourishment for optimal health and well-being.

The sign opposite a planet's domicile, where the planet is considered weakened and uncomfortable. A planet in detriment must work harder to express its nature, such as Mars in Libra or Venus in Scorpio.

A wealth-producing combination in Vedic astrology formed when lords of specific houses (especially the 2nd, 5th, 9th, and 11th) combine by conjunction, aspect, or exchange. The number and strength of dhana yogas in a chart indicate the native's potential for financial prosperity. Multiple dhana yogas amplify the effect.

The twenty-third nakshatra in Vedic astrology, spanning 23 degrees 20 minutes of Capricorn to 6 degrees 40 minutes of Aquarius. Its ruling deity is the Vasus (eight elemental gods), and its planetary ruler is Mars. It is associated with wealth, rhythm, music, and abundance.
How the body is designed to take in and process food. Determined by Design Sun/Earth Color and Tone, a key component of PHS.

The normal forward movement of a planet through the zodiac signs in their natural order. A planet in direct motion is considered to be functioning normally and expressing its energies in a straightforward, outward manner.
The Not-Self theme of the Reflector Type. When making decisions too quickly without waiting a full lunar cycle, disappointment results.

The planet that rules the sign another planet occupies, thereby having authority over that planet. For example, if Venus is in Capricorn, Saturn is the dispositor of Venus because Saturn rules Capricorn.

A birth chart where the Sun is above the horizon, indicating a daytime birth. In a diurnal chart, the Sun, Jupiter, and Saturn belong to the diurnal sect and tend to express their energies more favorably.

A system of sub-charts in Vedic astrology created by dividing each zodiac sign into smaller segments, with each division mapped to a new chart. There are 16 primary divisional charts (Shodashavarga), each revealing specific life areas such as marriage (D9), career (D10), or parents (D12). They provide a layered, detailed analysis that supplements the birth chart.
The sign a planet rules and where it is considered most at home and powerful. A planet in its domicile can express its nature freely and effectively, such as Mars in Aries or Venus in Taurus.

An astrological chart created by rotating the entire natal chart so that the north lunar node becomes 0 degrees Aries. All other planets and points shift by the same amount, revealing what is considered the soul's blueprint or pre-incarnation intent. It is used to explore karmic patterns and is often compared with the natal chart for deeper insight.

A zodiac framework anchored to the north lunar node rather than the vernal equinox or fixed stars. The north node defines 0 degrees Aries, and all planetary positions are recalculated relative to this point. It is interpreted as representing the soul level of the chart, distinct from the personality-level tropical or sidereal placements.

A divisional chart in Vedic astrology created by dividing each sign into three equal parts of 10 degrees each. It is primarily used to examine siblings, courage, and communication abilities. The drekkana also has applications in assessing the nature of illness in medical astrology.

The 6th, 8th, and 12th houses in Vedic astrology, traditionally considered challenging or inauspicious placements. They relate to enemies and disease (6th), sudden upheaval and transformation (8th), and loss and liberation (12th). Planets placed in dusthana houses face obstacles but can also produce spiritual growth and hidden strength.
One of the four classical elements in astrology, encompassing Taurus, Virgo, and Capricorn. Earth signs are associated with practicality, material concerns, stability, and grounded energy.
A concept related to sleep states where the body processes daily conditioning. Represents the grounded, physical realm of experience in the sleep cycle.

A period of approximately 34 to 38 days occurring twice a year when the Sun is close enough to the lunar nodes for eclipses to take place. Eclipse seasons are considered times of accelerated change, fated developments, and heightened sensitivity to the nodal axis themes.
Found exclusively in Manifestors with the Heart Center connected directly to the Throat. Decisions are made through willpower expressed directly through speech.
A rare authority found only in Projectors with the Heart Center connected to the G Center. Correct decisions come from listening to what they say about their desires when invited.

A branch of astrology focused on selecting the most favorable time to begin an important activity or event. Electional astrology applies chart analysis in reverse, choosing planetary alignments that support the desired outcome for weddings, business launches, surgeries, and other significant undertakings.
When one person has a Gate on one end of a channel and another has the Gate on the other end, completing the channel. Creates powerful attraction and shared energy.
The most common inner authority, found in approximately 50% of the population. The Solar Plexus operates as a wave that must be ridden over time. The key principle is there is no truth in the now.
The spatial awareness aspect of Variable in Human Design, derived from the bottom-left Arrow. It indicates the type of physical environment where you thrive and operate most correctly.

A table or reference listing the daily positions of celestial bodies over a period of time. Astrologers use an ephemeris to look up planetary positions for chart calculation, transit tracking, and predictive work.

A house system where each house spans exactly 30 degrees starting from the Ascendant degree. The Midheaven floats freely and may fall in a house other than the 10th. It is valued for its simplicity and works well at all latitudes.
BG5 Career Type equivalent of the Reflector. Samples and assesses work environment health, providing valuable organizational feedback.

A modern astrological approach developed primarily by Jeffrey Wolf Green and Steven Forrest that interprets the birth chart through the lens of soul evolution and reincarnation. It emphasizes the lunar nodes (especially Pluto's relationship to them) as indicators of past-life patterns and current evolutionary intentions. The approach focuses on growth, choice, and the soul's ongoing development across lifetimes.

A sign where a planet is honored and elevated, functioning with special strength though in a different manner than in domicile. For example, the Sun is exalted in Aries and Saturn is exalted in Libra.
BG5 Career Type equivalent of the Manifesting Generator. Combines sustainable life force with ability to initiate, fast and multi-capable.

A system of essential dignity that divides each zodiac sign into three equal 10-degree segments, each ruled by a different planet. Faces or decans represent the weakest level of essential dignity and add subtle coloring to a planet's expression within a sign.

An ayanamsa developed by Western sidereal astrologers Cyril Fagan and Donald Bradley in the mid-20th century. It differs from the Lahiri ayanamsa by approximately 1 degree and is the standard reference point for Western sidereal astrology. Its value is based on the synetic vernal point derived from ancient Egyptian and Babylonian records.

The sign opposite a planet's exaltation, where the planet is considered at its weakest essential dignity. A planet in fall struggles to express its best qualities, such as the Sun in Libra or Saturn in Aries.
One of six Motivation types (Color 1). Drives awareness through alertness and caution, a survival-oriented focus.
One of six Cognition types (Tone 5). Processes information through emotional and sensory impressions, sensitivity to atmosphere.

One of the two polarities in astrology, assigned to earth and water signs. Feminine or yin signs (Taurus, Cancer, Virgo, Scorpio, Capricorn, Pisces) are considered introverted, receptive, and inwardly directed.

A planet in its own domicile that sits at the end of the dispositor chain, meaning all other planets ultimately trace their rulership back to it. A chart with a final dispositor has a single planet that anchors and unifies the entire chart's energy.

A medieval timing technique that assigns sequential planetary rulership periods across a person's life, with different sequences for diurnal and nocturnal charts. Each planet rules a multi-year period and collaborates with sub-period rulers, coloring the themes and experiences of those years.
One of the four classical elements in astrology, encompassing Aries, Leo, and Sagittarius. Fire signs are associated with energy, enthusiasm, inspiration, and action-oriented behavior.

The lunar phase when the Moon is 90 degrees ahead of the Sun and appears half-illuminated. The first quarter represents a crisis of action, requiring decisions, adjustments, and commitment to overcome early obstacles in the lunar cycle.
One of four animal categories in the Variable system. A specific pattern of strategic and receptive orientations influencing dietary requirements and information processing.

One of the three modalities in astrology, encompassing Taurus, Leo, Scorpio, and Aquarius. Fixed signs sustain and stabilize energy in the middle of each season, representing determination and persistence.

Stars and stellar objects beyond the solar system that are used as interpretive points in astrology, particularly in traditional and medieval practice. Despite their name, fixed stars precess slowly through the tropical zodiac at about one degree every 72 years, and they add powerful layers of meaning when conjunct natal planets or angles.

One of the four Royal Stars of Persia, located at approximately 4 degrees Pisces in tropical astrology. Fomalhaut is associated with idealism, mystical vision, fame, and great potential, with the condition that success requires maintaining integrity and avoiding corruption.
The Not-Self theme of the Generator Type. When initiating rather than responding, the result is frustration from using Sacral energy on wrong things.

The lunar phase when the Sun and Moon are in opposition, approximately 180 degrees apart, and the Moon appears fully illuminated. The full moon represents culmination, completion, heightened emotions, and the manifestation of intentions set at the previous new moon.
The center of identity, love, and direction. It houses the Magnetic Monopole, which holds together our illusion of separateness and guides us along our correct geometry in life.

A beneficial yoga in Vedic astrology formed when Jupiter is in a kendra (1st, 4th, 7th, or 10th house) from the Moon. The name means 'elephant-lion,' signifying strength and nobility. It bestows wisdom, reputation, and the capacity to influence and lead others.

The junction points between water and fire signs in Vedic astrology (Pisces-Aries, Cancer-Leo, Scorpio-Sagittarius), considered spiritually sensitive and karmically intense zones. Planets or the Ascendant placed in gandanta degrees (the last 3 degrees 20 minutes of a water sign or first 3 degrees 20 minutes of a fire sign) indicate deep karmic knots. These points are especially significant for the Moon's placement at birth.

One of 64 energy points in the bodygraph, corresponding to the 64 hexagrams of the I Ching. Each Gate carries a specific theme and energy. When two Gates at opposite ends of a Channel are both defined, they form a complete Channel.
The third sign of the zodiac, spanning 60 to 90 degrees of the ecliptic. Gemini is a mutable air sign ruled by Mercury, associated with communication, curiosity, and adaptability.

One of the five Types in Human Design. Generators have a defined Sacral Center and are designed to respond to life rather than initiate. They have sustainable life force energy when engaged in work they love.
Large-scale evolutionary cycles affecting humanity, determined by slow-moving outer planets through the Rave Mandala gates.

The Sanskrit term for a planet or celestial body used in Vedic astrology. The traditional scheme includes nine grahas: Sun, Moon, Mars, Mercury, Jupiter, Venus, Saturn, Rahu, and Ketu. The word graha literally means 'that which seizes or grasps.'

An aspect pattern formed when four planets create two oppositions that square each other, forming a cross or square shape in the chart. The grand cross represents intense tension, tremendous drive, and significant challenges that demand constant effort to manage.

An aspect pattern formed when three planets are each approximately 120 degrees apart, creating an equilateral triangle in the chart. A grand trine indicates exceptional ease and flow in one element, though it can also represent complacency if not actively utilized.
One of six Motivation types (Color 5). Drives awareness through duty and responsibility, attuned to obligations.
An activated Gate without its corresponding harmonic Gate to form a channel. Creates an electromagnetic pull toward people who carry the other half.

The thirteenth nakshatra in Vedic astrology, spanning 10 degrees to 23 degrees 20 minutes of Virgo. Its ruling deity is Savitar (the Sun as creative force), and its planetary ruler is the Moon. It is associated with skillfulness, craftsmanship, and the ability to manifest goals.

A condition of accidental dignity where a planet is in agreement with the chart's sect, the correct hemisphere, and a sign of matching gender. A planet in hayz is considered to be in its most comfortable and effective condition, fully aligned with its nature.
A pressure center at the top of the Bodygraph that creates mental pressure to think, question, and seek inspiration. It is the center of inspiration and mental anxiety.

The motor center of willpower, self-worth, and the material world, also called the Ego Center. Governs the capacity to make and keep promises and compete. Only about 30% of the population has it defined.

The first visible appearance of a star or planet on the eastern horizon just before sunrise, after a period of invisibility due to conjunction with the Sun. The heliacal rising of Sirius was critically important in ancient Egyptian astrology, and heliacal phases of planets carry special significance in traditional practice.
A six-line figure from the I Ching forming the basis of each of the 64 Gates. Consists of two trigrams creating six lines corresponding to the Lines of each Gate.
One of six Motivation types (Color 2). Drives awareness through optimism and trust in positive outcomes.

A branch of astrology that answers specific questions by casting a chart for the moment the question is clearly understood by the astrologer. Horary uses strict traditional rules and dignities to provide concrete yes-or-no answers and detailed descriptions of outcomes.
The ancient Chinese Book of Changes providing the 64 hexagrams for the 64 Gates. Ra Uru Hu synthesized it with astrology, Kabbalah, and the Chakra system.

The official boundaries of the 88 constellations as defined by the International Astronomical Union in 1930. In true sidereal astrology, these boundaries determine when the Sun, Moon, and planets transition from one constellation-sign to another. Because constellations vary in size, some zodiacal constellations span as little as 7 degrees (Scorpius) or as much as 44 degrees (Virgo).

The lowest point of the ecliptic in a birth chart, marking the cusp of the 4th House and directly opposing the Midheaven. The IC represents private life, emotional roots, family heritage, and the foundation of one's inner world.
The primary life purpose theme in Human Design, determined by the Sun and Earth positions in both Personality and Design. There are 192 basic Incarnation Crosses, each carrying a unique life theme.
Contains the Knowing and Centering Circuits. Theme is empowerment and mutation. Operates in pulses of melancholy and inspiration.

The moment a planet enters a new zodiac sign, marking a shift in how that planet's energy is expressed. Ingresses are significant in mundane astrology, where the Sun's entry into cardinal signs (the equinoxes and solstices) is used to forecast seasonal and national themes.
One of six Cognition types (Tone 4). Processes information through internal imagery, visualization, and dreams.
One of six Motivation types (Color 6). Drives awareness through pure, unfiltered perception without agenda.
Four special channels not belonging to any circuit group. Self-oriented, focused on survival: 20-57, 20-34, 10-57, and 34-57.

A condition in certain house systems where an entire zodiac sign is contained within a house without appearing on either cusp. Intercepted signs and their rulers are considered blocked or hidden, requiring extra effort to access their energies consciously.
BG5 Career Type equivalent of the Manifestor. Initiator designed to inform others, excelling in roles requiring independent vision.

A standard astronomical reference point defined as January 1, 2000, at 12:00 Terrestrial Time. It provides a fixed coordinate system for measuring celestial positions without ongoing precessional drift. In J2000-based astrology, planetary positions are calculated relative to this fixed reference frame.

A zodiac system that fixes the sign boundaries at their positions as of the J2000 epoch (January 1, 2000). Unlike the tropical zodiac, it does not shift with the seasons, and unlike the traditional sidereal zodiac, it does not rely on a specific star as its anchor point. It provides a stable, astronomically defined coordinate framework for chart calculation.

An asteroid representing committed partnerships, marriage, and the balance of power in relationships. Juno indicates what a person needs in a long-term partner and how they navigate themes of loyalty, equality, and compromise.
The planet of expansion, growth, abundance, and wisdom in astrology. Jupiter rules Sagittarius and traditionally co-rules Pisces, representing optimism, higher learning, and good fortune.
Approximately every 12 years. Each return brings expansion, opportunity, and a new growth chapter at ages 12, 24, 36, 48, 60.

The eighteenth nakshatra in Vedic astrology, spanning 16 degrees 40 minutes to 30 degrees of Scorpio. Its ruling deity is Indra (king of the gods), and its planetary ruler is Mercury. It is associated with seniority, protective power, and the responsibilities that come with authority.

The traditional Hindu system of astrology, also known as Vedic astrology, rooted in ancient Indian scriptures. It uses the Sidereal zodiac and incorporates unique techniques such as nakshatras, dashas, and divisional charts. Jyotish translates to 'science of light' in Sanskrit.

A condition in Vedic astrology where all seven visible planets are hemmed between Rahu and Ketu in the birth chart. It is believed to create recurring obstacles, karmic intensity, and sudden reversals in the native's life. The severity varies depending on which houses Rahu and Ketu occupy.

Half of a tithi in the Vedic calendar, representing the time it takes for the Moon to gain 6 degrees from the Sun. There are 11 karanas, with four fixed and seven rotating through the lunar month. Karanas are one of the five elements of the panchang and influence the quality of time for initiating activities.

The 1st, 4th, 7th, and 10th houses in Vedic astrology, corresponding to the four angles of the chart. They are the pillars of the horoscope and represent the most visible and action-oriented areas of life. Strong planets in kendra houses give the native power, stability, and the ability to manifest results.

The south lunar node in Vedic astrology, considered a shadow planet that represents spiritual liberation, detachment, past-life mastery, and dissolution of material attachments. Ketu's house and sign placement indicate areas of innate skill but also loss, apathy, or renunciation. It is always exactly opposite Rahu in the chart.
One of six Environment types (Color 3). A warm, communal gathering space centered around nourishment and transformation.

An aspect pattern formed when a grand trine has one planet that also opposes a fourth planet, which sextiles the other two trine planets. The kite adds focus and direction to the ease of a grand trine, channeling talent into productive achievement through the opposition.

A house system developed by Walter Koch that divides the houses based on the time it takes for the Ascendant degree to reach each house cusp. It is similar to Placidus but uses a different method of time-based division. Like Placidus, it becomes problematic at very high latitudes.

An ayanamsa developed by K.S. Krishnamurti as part of his Krishnamurti Paddhati (KP) system of Vedic astrology. It is very close to the Lahiri ayanamsa but differs by a few arc-minutes. The KP system uses this ayanamsa along with a unique sub-lord system based on nakshatra subdivisions for precise prediction.

The third nakshatra in Vedic astrology, spanning 26 degrees 40 minutes of Aries to 10 degrees of Taurus. Its ruling deity is Agni (god of fire), and its planetary ruler is the Sun. It is associated with purification, sharpness, and cutting through illusion.

The Sanskrit term for the Ascendant or rising sign in Vedic astrology, representing the degree of the zodiac rising on the eastern horizon at the time of birth. It is the most important point in the Vedic chart, determining the arrangement of all houses. The lagna signifies the native's physical body, personality, and overall life direction.

The most commonly used ayanamsa in Vedic astrology, officially adopted by the Indian government in 1956 based on the work of astronomer N.C. Lahiri. It places the starting point of the sidereal zodiac based on the star Spica (Chitra) at exactly 0 degrees Libra. As of 2024, the Lahiri ayanamsa is approximately 24 degrees 12 minutes.

The lunar phase when the Moon is 90 degrees behind the Sun and appears half-illuminated on the left side. The last quarter represents a crisis of consciousness, prompting reflection, re-evaluation, and the release of what no longer serves.
In Variable notation, a left-pointing arrow indicates a strategic, focused, and active quality. Represents a consistent, reliable pattern operating through concentration.
The fifth sign of the zodiac, spanning 120 to 150 degrees of the ecliptic. Leo is a fixed fire sign ruled by the Sun, associated with creativity, self-expression, and leadership.
The seventh sign of the zodiac, spanning 180 to 210 degrees of the ecliptic. Libra is a cardinal air sign ruled by Venus, associated with relationships, balance, and justice.
One of six Determination types (Color 6). Lighting conditions during meals affect how the body digests correctly.
One of the dream states in Human Design's understanding of sleep. Associated with the first phase of sleep where consciousness begins to withdraw from the waking state.

One of six positions within each Gate, corresponding to the six lines of an I Ching hexagram. Each Line carries a distinct theme: 1-Investigator, 2-Hermit, 3-Martyr, 4-Opportunist, 5-Heretic, 6-Role Model.
The foundation of the hexagram. An intrinsic need to research and build a secure base of knowledge before feeling comfortable. Security comes from thorough understanding.
Represents natural talent and the need for withdrawal. Has gifts they may not recognize and requires time alone to develop abilities. Needs to be called out by others.
The process of trial and error. Learns through direct experience, discovering what works and what does not by bumping into life. Resilience from practical wisdom.
The foundation of the upper trigram and the power of relationships. Finds opportunities through personal network rather than strangers.
Represents universalization and the projection field. Others project expectations and savior qualities. Seen as a practical problem-solver who can deliver universal solutions.
A unique three-phase life process: trial and error until about 30, observing from the roof until about 50, then stepping down as a living example of authenticity.

The Sun and Moon, the two brightest celestial bodies as seen from Earth. In astrology, the luminaries are considered the most important chart factors, representing the core self (Sun) and the emotional nature (Moon).
Unique to Reflectors, who have no defined centers. They are designed to wait through a full Moon cycle, approximately 28.5 days, before making major decisions.

A full moon that occurs near one of the lunar nodes, where the Earth passes between the Sun and Moon, casting its shadow on the Moon. Lunar eclipses are associated with emotional revelations, endings, release, and the culmination of themes that have been building over months.

A chart cast for the moment each month when the transiting Moon returns to its natal position. The lunar return chart forecasts emotional themes and events for the approximately 27.3-day lunar cycle ahead.
Monthly cycle every 27.3 days when the Moon returns to its natal position. Snapshot of emotional themes for the coming month.

The tenth nakshatra in Vedic astrology, spanning 0 to 13 degrees 20 minutes of Leo. Its ruling deity is the Pitris (ancestral spirits), and its planetary ruler is Ketu. It is associated with royalty, authority, and honoring one's lineage.
A unique particle in the G Center holding together the Personality and Design Crystals. Functions as a driver pulling the individual along their correct geometry. Only attracts, never repels.

The major planetary period in the Vimshottari Dasha system, during which one graha dominates the native's experience. Each mahadasha lasts a specific number of years (ranging from 6 years for the Sun to 20 years for Venus). It sets the overarching theme for that chapter of life.

A classification for planets considered to produce challenging or difficult outcomes in traditional astrology. Saturn is the Greater Malefic and Mars is the Lesser Malefic, generally bringing restriction, conflict, or hardship to the areas they influence.

A Hellenistic concept where a malefic planet (Mars or Saturn) damages the condition of another planet or house through hard aspect, conjunction, or adverse configuration. Specific forms include being overcome, enclosure, and striking with a ray. It is the negative counterpart to bonification in the Hellenistic system of planetary condition.
One of four animal categories in the Variable system, determined by the four arrow orientations. Each combination influences cognitive architecture and dietary needs.
The circular wheel chart that shows the 64 Gates arranged around the zodiac. The Mandala displays planetary positions in the context of the I Ching hexagrams and their relationship to the bodygraph.

A condition in Vedic astrology caused by Mars occupying the 1st, 2nd, 4th, 7th, 8th, or 12th house from the Ascendant, Moon, or Venus. It is traditionally believed to create challenges in marriage and partnerships. Various cancellation conditions can mitigate its effects.

One of the five Types in Human Design. Manifesting Generators have a defined Sacral Center and a motor connected to the Throat. They are designed to respond and then inform before acting, with the ability to move quickly between tasks.
One of the five Types in Human Design. Manifestors have a motor center connected to the Throat but no defined Sacral. They are designed to initiate and inform others before acting.
One of six Environment types (Color 2). A busy, commercial space. Those with this Environment thrive in bustling, populated areas.
The planet of action, desire, aggression, and physical energy in astrology. Mars rules Aries and traditionally co-rules Scorpio, representing drive, ambition, and how a person asserts themselves.

One of the two polarities in astrology, assigned to fire and air signs. Masculine or yang signs (Aries, Gemini, Leo, Libra, Sagittarius, Aquarius) are considered extroverted, active, and outwardly directed.
Found in Projectors with no centers defined below the Throat except possibly the Root. These individuals must discuss decisions with trusted others and pay attention to the environment.
The planet of communication, thought, and short-distance travel in astrology. Mercury rules Gemini and Virgo and governs how a person processes information, learns, and expresses ideas.
The highest point of the ecliptic in a birth chart, marking the cusp of the 10th House. The Midheaven represents public image, career direction, reputation, and one's highest aspirations in life.

One of the two luminaries in astrology, representing emotions, instincts, habits, and the inner self. The Moon changes signs approximately every two and a half days, making it the fastest-moving body used in chart interpretation.
One of six Environment types (Color 4). An elevated space with broad view. Those with this Environment thrive in high places.

The fifth nakshatra in Vedic astrology, spanning 23 degrees 20 minutes of Taurus to 6 degrees 40 minutes of Gemini. Its ruling deity is Soma (the Moon god), and its planetary ruler is Mars. It is associated with searching, curiosity, and the pursuit of knowledge.

The nineteenth nakshatra in Vedic astrology, spanning 0 to 13 degrees 20 minutes of Sagittarius. Its ruling deity is Nirriti (goddess of dissolution), and its planetary ruler is Ketu. It is associated with getting to the root of things, uprooting, and foundational transformation.

The branch of astrology concerned with world events, politics, nations, and collective experience rather than individual lives. Mundane astrology examines ingress charts, eclipses, and major planetary cycles to understand historical patterns and forecast geopolitical trends.

One of the three modalities in astrology, encompassing Gemini, Virgo, Sagittarius, and Pisces. Mutable signs adapt and transition energy at the end of each season, representing flexibility and change.

A condition where two planets each occupy a sign ruled by the other, creating a reciprocal exchange of dignity. For example, Mars in Taurus and Venus in Aries are in mutual reception by domicile, which strengthens both planets by giving them a cooperative link.

An aspect pattern formed by two oppositions connected by sextiles and trines, creating a rectangle shape in the chart. The mystic rectangle blends the tension of oppositions with the ease of trines and sextiles, offering practical outlets for resolving internal conflicts.

Each nakshatra is divided into four equal quarters called padas, each spanning 3 degrees 20 minutes. Each pada corresponds to a sign of the navamsa chart, adding a layer of nuance to the nakshatra's expression. The 108 total padas (27 nakshatras x 4) map to the 12 navamsa signs in a repeating cycle.

A map of the sky at the exact moment and location of a person's birth, showing the positions of all planets, signs, and houses. The natal chart is the foundational tool of astrology, serving as a blueprint for an individual's personality, potential, and life themes.

The most important divisional chart in Vedic astrology, created by dividing each sign into nine equal parts of 3 degrees 20 minutes. It is primarily used to assess the strength of planets, the quality of marriage, and the deeper spiritual purpose of the native. A planet's navamsa placement can confirm or modify its promise in the birth chart.
One of six Motivation types (Color 4). Drives awareness through necessity and practical requirement.

The planet of dreams, illusion, spirituality, and transcendence in astrology. Neptune is the modern ruler of Pisces and takes approximately 165 years to orbit the Sun, spending about 14 years in each sign.
Subatomic particles streaming through everything, carrying infinitesimally small mass and therefore information. Approximately 70% of the conditioning neutrino stream comes from the Sun.

The lunar phase when the Sun and Moon are conjunct in the same zodiac sign, making the Moon invisible from Earth. The new moon represents new beginnings, fresh starts, and the planting of seeds for intentions that will develop over the coming lunar cycle.
The definition type of Reflectors, who have no defined centers. All nine centers are open, making them completely receptive to conditioning from their environment.

A birth chart where the Sun is below the horizon, indicating a nighttime birth. In a nocturnal chart, the Moon, Venus, and Mars belong to the nocturnal sect and tend to express their energies more favorably.
Every 18.6 years when Lunar Nodes return to natal positions. Activates themes of life direction and purpose.

One of the two lunar nodes, representing the point where the Moon's orbit crosses the ecliptic moving northward. The North Node indicates karmic direction, soul growth, and qualities a person is developing in this lifetime.
The conditioned identity arising from decisions made from open and undefined centers rather than Authority. The false self shaped by external conditioning.
Large group dynamic for organizations of 16 or more, representing how large groups function as a transpersonal entity with emergent properties.
A center with no activated Gates at all, completely receptive to outside conditioning. Amplifies energy received and represents areas of potential wisdom.

A constellation that lies along the ecliptic between Scorpius and Sagittarius, sometimes proposed as a 13th zodiac sign. The Sun passes through Ophiuchus from approximately November 30 to December 18 based on IAU boundaries. It is not recognized in traditional Western or Vedic astrology but is included in 13-sign and some true sidereal systems.
A major aspect formed when two planets are approximately 180 degrees apart in the zodiac. Oppositions represent tension, polarity, and the need to balance or integrate two competing areas of life.

The degree of deviation allowed from an exact aspect angle for the aspect to still be considered active. For example, a conjunction is exact at 0 degrees, but with an orb of 8 degrees, planets within 8 degrees of each other would still be considered conjunct.

A planetary condition where a planet's declination exceeds 23 degrees 27 minutes, beyond the Sun's maximum declination. Out of bounds planets are considered to operate outside normal rules and boundaries, expressing their energies in extreme, unconventional, or exceptional ways.
One of six Cognition types (Tone 3). Processes information through direct visual observation of the external environment.

An asteroid named after Pallas Athena, representing wisdom, strategy, creative intelligence, and pattern recognition in astrology. Pallas indicates how a person perceives patterns, fights for justice, and applies strategic thinking.

A set of five powerful yogas in Vedic astrology formed when Mars, Mercury, Jupiter, Venus, or Saturn occupies its own sign or exaltation sign in a kendra house. Each yoga has a specific name: Ruchaka (Mars), Bhadra (Mercury), Hamsa (Jupiter), Malavya (Venus), and Shasha (Saturn). These yogas produce individuals of exceptional character and achievement.

The traditional Hindu almanac based on five elements of time: tithi (lunar day), vara (weekday), nakshatra (lunar mansion), yoga (Sun-Moon angular relationship), and karana (half tithi). It is used daily in Vedic astrology for muhurta (electional timing), festival scheduling, and determining the quality of any given moment.

An Arabic Part calculated from the positions of the Sun, Moon, and Ascendant that indicates areas of natural talent and worldly success. The formula differs for diurnal and nocturnal charts, reflecting the importance of sect in traditional astrology.

An Arabic Part calculated as the reverse formula of the Part of Fortune, switching the positions of the Sun and Moon in the calculation. The Part of Spirit relates to the mind, intellect, career actions, and matters of purpose, will, and conscious direction in life.

An aspect that is exact or within one degree of exactitude. Partile aspects are considered especially powerful because the planetary energies are at their tightest connection, producing the most concentrated and noticeable effects.
The Signature theme of the Manifestor Type. When correctly informing before initiating, they encounter less resistance and experience deep peace.
A transpersonal form that emerges when three to five people come together. The Penta has its own mechanical dynamics that influence how small groups function, communicate, and work together.
One of three Penta functional areas governing how a group integrates new members, ideas, and resources.
One of three Penta functional areas governing how information flows, ideas are shared, and verbal exchange maintained.
One of three Penta functional areas governing resources, logistics, task organization, and practical workflow.

A planetary condition where a planet has no essential dignity in its current sign position, meaning it holds no rulership, exaltation, triplicity, term, or face. A peregrine planet is considered a wanderer without resources, weakened and less able to act effectively.
One of six Perspective types (Color 6). Sees the world through a deeply subjective, individual lens.

The fast-moving planets closest to the Sun that shape individual personality traits. The personal planets include the Sun, Moon, Mercury, Venus, and Mars, and they change signs frequently enough to differentiate individuals born close together in time.

The conscious side of a Human Design chart, calculated from the moment of birth. Personality activations are shown in black on the bodygraph. These represent qualities the person is consciously aware of and identifies with.
The consciousness aspect of the self, entering the body at birth. Corresponds to the black activations on the right side of the Bodygraph. Calculated from the exact moment of birth.
The visual awareness aspect of Variable in Human Design, derived from the bottom-right Arrow. It indicates how you are designed to see and observe the world around you.

A Hellenistic astrological concept referring to the visible appearance or disappearance of a planet relative to the Sun's beams (typically within 15 degrees of the Sun). A planet making its phasis (emerging from the Sun's rays) was considered to gain prominence and power. Planets under the beams were considered weakened or hidden.
Primary Health System, the body-focused branch of Variables addressing how to eat and digest. Derived from Design Sun/Earth Tone and Color.

The twelfth sign of the zodiac, spanning 330 to 360 degrees of the ecliptic. Pisces is a mutable water sign traditionally ruled by Jupiter and modernly by Neptune, associated with intuition, compassion, and transcendence.

The most widely used house system in modern Western astrology, developed by the 17th-century mathematician Placidus de Titis. It divides houses based on the time it takes for each degree of the ecliptic to rise from the nadir to the horizon. It can produce unequal house sizes and becomes unreliable at extreme latitudes.

The assignment of each day of the week to one of the seven classical planets, a system reflected in the names of the days in many languages. Sunday is ruled by the Sun, Monday by the Moon, Tuesday by Mars, Wednesday by Mercury, Thursday by Jupiter, Friday by Venus, and Saturday by Saturn.

A traditional system that divides each day and night into segments, each ruled by one of the seven classical planets in a fixed sequence. Planetary hours are used in electional and horary astrology to select auspicious times for activities aligned with a specific planet's nature.

A traditional system assigning each of the seven classical planets a house where it rejoices and expresses itself most naturally. For example, Mercury rejoices in the 1st House, the Moon in the 3rd, Venus in the 5th, Mars in the 6th, the Sun in the 9th, Jupiter in the 11th, and Saturn in the 12th.

The rate at which a planet moves through the zodiac, which varies throughout its orbit. In traditional astrology, a fast-moving planet is considered more active and capable, while a slow-moving planet may indicate delays or reduced effectiveness.

The planet of transformation, power, death and rebirth, and the unconscious in astrology. Pluto is the modern ruler of Scorpio and has an irregular orbit of approximately 248 years, spending between 12 and 31 years in each sign.

A house system attributed to the 3rd-century philosopher Porphyry of Tyre that trisects the arcs between the four angles (Ascendant, IC, Descendant, MC). Each quadrant is divided into three equal parts to create the intermediate house cusps. It is one of the simplest quadrant-based house systems.
One of six Perspective types (Color 2). Sees the world through potential and opportunity.
One of six Perspective types (Color 3). Sees the world through influence and control dynamics.

The sub-sub-period within an antardasha in the Vimshottari Dasha system. It provides an even finer level of timing by further dividing each antardasha into nine smaller periods. Pratyantar dashas are used for precise event prediction.

The slow westward drift of the equinox points along the ecliptic caused by the wobble of Earth's rotational axis. This cycle takes approximately 25,772 years to complete and is the reason the Tropical and Sidereal zodiacs diverge over time.

One of the oldest predictive techniques in astrology, based on the diurnal rotation of the Earth rather than planetary motion. Primary directions measure the time it takes for a natal planet to reach the position of another planet or angle through the Earth's daily rotation, using approximately one degree equals one year.
One of six Perspective types (Color 5). Sees the world through likelihood and patterns, calculating odds.
A combination of two Lines (one conscious, one unconscious) that describes your costume in life and how you interact with others. There are 12 Profiles in Human Design, from 1/3 to 6/3.
The collective conditioning field from planetary movement through the Mandala. Defines the backdrop of collective experience at any given moment.

The Ascendant degree in the secondary progressed chart, which moves forward through the zodiac at a rate determined by the latitude and time of birth. When the progressed Ascendant changes signs, the native's outward persona and approach to life undergo a noticeable shift. It typically takes many years to progress through a single sign.

A chart derived from the natal chart using a symbolic time scale, most commonly secondary progressions where each day after birth equals one year of life. The progressed chart shows the gradual internal evolution of a person's character and life direction over time.

The moment when the progressed Moon returns to its exact natal position, occurring approximately every 27 to 28 years. It marks the beginning of a new emotional and developmental cycle, similar in significance to a Saturn return but focused on inner life and emotional maturity. The first progressed lunar return happens around age 27.

The position of the Moon in the secondary progressed chart, which moves approximately one degree per month of real time and completes a full zodiac cycle every 27 to 28 years. Its sign and house placement indicate the native's evolving emotional focus and inner needs. The progressed Moon's aspects to natal and progressed planets mark significant emotional turning points.
One of the five Types in Human Design. Projectors have no motor connected to the Throat and no defined Sacral. They are designed to wait for recognition and invitation before sharing their guidance.

An approach to astrology that integrates Jungian psychology and depth psychology with traditional chart interpretation. Pioneered by astrologers such as Dane Rudhyar, Liz Greene, and Howard Sasportas, it views the birth chart as a map of the psyche rather than a deterministic fate map. It emphasizes archetypes, individuation, and personal growth.

The seventh nakshatra in Vedic astrology, spanning 20 degrees of Gemini to 3 degrees 20 minutes of Cancer. Its ruling deity is Aditi (mother of the gods), and its planetary ruler is Jupiter. It is associated with restoration, renewal, and the return of light.

The twentieth nakshatra in Vedic astrology, spanning 13 degrees 20 minutes to 26 degrees 40 minutes of Sagittarius. Its ruling deity is Apas (goddess of water), and its planetary ruler is Venus. It is associated with invincibility, purification, and the power of rejuvenation.

The twenty-fifth nakshatra in Vedic astrology, spanning 20 degrees of Aquarius to 3 degrees 20 minutes of Pisces. Its ruling deity is Aja Ekapada (one-footed serpent), and its planetary ruler is Jupiter. It is associated with intensity, penance, and transformative spiritual fire.

The eleventh nakshatra in Vedic astrology, spanning 13 degrees 20 minutes to 26 degrees 40 minutes of Leo. Its ruling deity is Bhaga (god of fortune), and its planetary ruler is Venus. It is associated with pleasure, relaxation, creativity, and romantic fulfillment.

Specific navamsa divisions within certain signs that are considered especially auspicious and nourishing in Vedic astrology. A planet placed in a pushkara navamsa gains additional benefic strength regardless of its other dignities. These special divisions enhance the planet's ability to deliver positive results.

The eighth nakshatra in Vedic astrology, spanning 3 degrees 20 minutes to 16 degrees 40 minutes of Cancer. Its ruling deity is Brihaspati (Jupiter as divine teacher), and its planetary ruler is Saturn. It is considered one of the most auspicious nakshatras, associated with nourishment and spiritual growth.
The rarest definition type with four separate areas. Less than 1% of the population. Very fixed and resilient nature, needing time for complex energy processing.
A chart view in Genetic Matrix that shows both the Personality (black/conscious) and Design (red/unconscious) activations together on the bodygraph, giving the complete picture of someone's design.
Second of four Quarters. Purpose fulfilled through Form. Energy of building, structuring, and manifesting.
Third of four Quarters. Purpose fulfilled through Bonding. Energy of relationships and partnerships.
First of four Quarters in the HD Wheel. Purpose fulfilled through Mind. Energy of beginning and initiating new cycles.
Fourth of four Quarters. Purpose fulfilled through Transformation. Energy of change, evolution, and transcendence.

An aspect formed when two planets are approximately 150 degrees apart in the zodiac. The quincunx creates a sense of awkwardness or misalignment between two areas of life that require ongoing adjustment and adaptation.

A minor aspect formed when two planets are approximately 72 degrees apart in the zodiac. The quintile is associated with creative talent, unique gifts, and the ability to produce something original from the combination of planetary energies.

The north lunar node in Vedic astrology, considered a shadow planet (chhaya graha) that represents worldly desire, obsession, amplification, and unconventional pursuits. Rahu's house and sign placement indicate areas where the native experiences intense craving and karmic growth through unfamiliar territory. It is always exactly opposite Ketu in the chart.

A powerful combination in Vedic astrology formed when lords of kendra (angular) and trikona (trinal) houses connect through conjunction, mutual aspect, or exchange. It signifies rise in status, power, and authority in the native's life. The strength of the yoga depends on the dignity and placement of the planets involved.

An ayanamsa developed by the prominent Indian astrologer B.V. Raman, differing from the Lahiri ayanamsa by approximately 2 degrees. It is based on Raman's independent astronomical calculations of the precessional rate. It is less widely used than Lahiri but has a dedicated following among practitioners of Raman's approach.

The Sanskrit term for a zodiac sign in Vedic astrology. There are 12 rashis, each spanning 30 degrees of the sidereal ecliptic. The rashis correspond to the same signs as Western astrology but are measured against the fixed stars rather than the seasons.
The technical name for an individual's Human Design Bodygraph. Calculated from birth data, displaying nine centers, 36 channels, and 64 Gates.
The beginning of the Human Design solar cycle when the Sun enters Gate 41 around January 22nd. Gate 41 is the initiating codon representing pressure to start new experiences.

One of the five Types in Human Design. Reflectors have no defined centers at all. They are designed to wait a full lunar cycle (28 days) before making major decisions, sampling and reflecting the energy around them.

A house system developed by the 15th-century astronomer Johannes Muller (Regiomontanus) that divides the celestial equator into equal 30-degree segments and projects them onto the ecliptic. It was the dominant system in European astrology from the Renaissance through the 17th century. It is still favored by some horary astrologers.

One of the four Royal Stars of Persia, located at approximately 0 degrees Virgo in modern tropical astrology. Regulus is the brightest star in the constellation Leo and is associated with glory, fame, leadership, and success, but with a warning against revenge.
One of four animal categories in the Variable system. A particular combination of left/right arrows shaping how one is designed to eat, think, and interact.

The apparent backward motion of a planet as observed from Earth, caused by differences in orbital speed. In astrology, retrograde periods are associated with review, revision, delays, and internalization of the planet's themes.

The period before and after a planet's retrograde when it traverses the same degrees it will revisit or has revisited during retrograde. The pre-shadow introduces themes that will be reviewed during retrograde, while the post-shadow completes the integration process.

The twenty-seventh and final nakshatra in Vedic astrology, spanning 16 degrees 40 minutes to 30 degrees of Pisces. Its ruling deity is Pushan (the nourisher and protector of travelers), and its planetary ruler is Mercury. It is associated with safe journeys, completion, and transcendence.
In Variable notation, a right-pointing arrow indicates a receptive, peripheral, and passive quality. Represents a flexible, sampling pattern through openness.

The fourth nakshatra in Vedic astrology, spanning 10 degrees to 23 degrees 20 minutes of Taurus. Its ruling deity is Brahma (the creator), and its planetary ruler is the Moon. It is associated with growth, beauty, fertility, and material abundance.
A pressure and motor center at the base of the Bodygraph creating adrenaline-based pressure to act and move forward. Fuels the drive to get things done.
Belongs to Generators and Manifesting Generators with a defined Sacral but undefined Solar Plexus. The Sacral responds in the moment with gut sounds or physical sensations indicating yes or no.
The powerhouse motor Center in the bodygraph, responsible for life force and workforce energy. A defined Sacral is what makes someone a Generator or Manifesting Generator Type.
The ninth sign of the zodiac, spanning 240 to 270 degrees of the ecliptic. Sagittarius is a mutable fire sign ruled by Jupiter, associated with exploration, philosophy, and higher learning.

A cycle of approximately 18 years, 11 days, and 8 hours after which nearly identical eclipses recur. Astrologers use Saros cycles to connect thematically related eclipses across decades, tracing long-term evolutionary patterns and recurring life themes.
The Signature theme of the Generator Type. When waiting to respond and using Sacral energy on things that light them up, the result is deep satisfaction.

The planet of structure, discipline, limitation, and responsibility in astrology. Saturn rules Capricorn and traditionally co-rules Aquarius, representing life lessons, boundaries, and maturation over time.

Aspects from one person's Saturn to another person's personal planets in synastry, associated with commitment, longevity, and karmic lessons in relationships. Saturn conjunctions and trines can provide stability and lasting bonds, while Saturn squares and oppositions may create feelings of restriction, criticism, or obligation. These contacts are often found in long-term partnerships.
Major life cycle around age 29-30 when transiting Saturn returns to its natal position. Marks maturity and often brings significant life restructuring.

The eighth sign of the zodiac, spanning 210 to 240 degrees of the ecliptic. Scorpio is a fixed water sign traditionally ruled by Mars and modernly by Pluto, associated with transformation, intensity, and depth.
Occurs around age 58-60. Marks elder wisdom, legacy review, and deepening relationship with life purpose.

A predictive technique where each day after birth symbolically represents one year of life. Secondary progressions track the slow internal evolution of the psyche, with the progressed Moon changing signs approximately every two and a half years and marking significant emotional shifts.

A traditional astrological concept that divides planets into two teams based on whether the chart is a day (diurnal) or night (nocturnal) birth. Sect determines which benefic and malefic planets are more supportive or challenging in a given chart, fundamentally shaping how planets behave.

The luminary that leads the sect of a chart. In a diurnal chart the sect light is the Sun, and in a nocturnal chart the sect light is the Moon. The sect light carries special importance in traditional techniques including profections and lots.
Found in Projectors with the G Center connected to the Throat. Decision-making comes through hearing oneself speak about identity and direction with trusted others.

A minor aspect formed when two planets are approximately 30 degrees apart in the zodiac. The semi-sextile connects adjacent signs with different elements and modalities, creating a subtle tension that requires conscious integration.

A minor aspect formed when two planets are approximately 45 degrees apart in the zodiac. The semi-square produces mild irritation and internal friction, similar to a square but less intense, prompting small but persistent adjustments.
The Cognition derived from Personality Sun/Earth Tone. The specific sensory mode for taking in information, operating beneath conscious awareness.

An aspect where the faster-moving planet has already passed the exact degree of the aspect and is moving away. Separating aspects are considered to be waning in influence, representing energy that has already peaked and is dissipating.

A minor aspect formed when two planets are approximately 135 degrees apart in the zodiac. The sesquiquadrate produces agitation and restlessness similar to a semi-square, often manifesting as external frustrations or compulsive behavior.
The foundational deconditioning timeline. Ra taught it takes seven years of living by Strategy and Authority to release conditioning at a cellular level.

A major aspect formed when two planets are approximately 60 degrees apart in the zodiac. Sextiles represent opportunity, cooperation, and gentle support that can be activated through conscious effort.
