A system for calculating planetary strength in Vedic astrology based on six sources of power: positional, directional, temporal, motional, natural, and aspectual strength. The total shadbala score determines how effectively a planet can deliver its results. It is measured in units called rupas or shashtiamsas.

The twenty-fourth nakshatra in Vedic astrology, spanning 6 degrees 40 minutes to 20 degrees of Aquarius. Its ruling deity is Varuna (god of cosmic waters), and its planetary ruler is Rahu. It is associated with healing, secrecy, and the ability to contain or veil something.
One of six Environment types (Color 6). A transitional space where two elements meet, such as land and water.

The twenty-second nakshatra in Vedic astrology, spanning 10 degrees to 23 degrees 20 minutes of Capricorn. Its ruling deity is Vishnu (the preserver), and its planetary ruler is the Moon. It is associated with listening, learning, and the transmission of knowledge.

A zodiac system based on the actual positions of fixed star constellations rather than the seasons. It accounts for the precession of the equinoxes using an ayanamsa correction and is the primary system used in Vedic astrology.
The feeling confirming a person is living correctly by their Type, Strategy, and Authority. Peace (Manifestor), Satisfaction (Generator), Success (Projector), Surprise (Reflector).
All defined centers are connected in one continuous circuit. About 41% of the population. Self-contained in energy flow, not depending on others to bridge gaps.

The brightest star in the night sky, located at approximately 14 degrees Cancer in tropical astrology. Sirius is associated with ambition, fame, devotion, wealth, and honor, and has been one of the most important stars in astrology since ancient Egyptian times.

The importance of sleeping alone in one's own aura, especially with undefined centers. During sleep, the body processes daily conditioning. Ra emphasized sleeping in another's aura deeply conditions open centers.
One of six Cognition types (Tone 1). Processes information through instinctive, primal sense of right or wrong.

Jupiter and Saturn, which orbit between the personal and transpersonal planets. The social planets represent societal themes, cultural values, and generational trends over shorter periods than the outer planets, with Jupiter spending about a year in each sign and Saturn about two and a half years.

A predictive technique that advances every planet and point in the natal chart by the same arc, which equals the distance the secondary progressed Sun has traveled from its natal position. This means all planets move at roughly one degree per year. Solar arc directions are valued for their simplicity and for timing major life events.

A predictive technique that advances every planet and point in the natal chart by the same arc, equal to the distance the progressed Sun has traveled. Solar arc directions produce one major activation per year and are widely used for timing significant life events.

A new moon that occurs near one of the lunar nodes, where the Moon passes between the Earth and Sun, partially or fully blocking sunlight. Solar eclipses are considered powerful catalysts for major new beginnings, fate-driven events, and significant turning points that unfold over six months to a year.

The emotional awareness Center in the bodygraph. When defined, it becomes the Emotional Authority, operating in a wave pattern. Decisions should be made over time, not in the heat of emotional highs or lows.

A chart cast for the exact moment each year when the transiting Sun returns to its natal position. The solar return chart is used to forecast themes, opportunities, and challenges for the year ahead, from one birthday to the next.
Annual cycle when the Sun returns to its exact natal position, on or near one's birthday. Reveals themes for the coming year.
One of six Determination types (Color 5). The acoustic environment during meals matters for proper digestion.

One of the two lunar nodes, representing the point where the Moon's orbit crosses the ecliptic moving southward. The South Node indicates past-life tendencies, innate gifts, and comfort zones that may need to be released for growth.

A bright fixed star in the constellation Virgo, located at approximately 24 degrees Libra in tropical astrology. Spica is considered one of the most fortunate stars, associated with brilliance, fame, wealth, artistic talent, and protection from harm.
The body consciousness Center in the bodygraph, responsible for intuition, instinct, taste, and immune system awareness. Splenic awareness is in-the-moment and does not repeat its signals.
Operates through the Spleen Center, providing instantaneous awareness rooted in survival instinct and intuition. It speaks only once and very quietly.
Two separate areas of definition not connected to each other. About 46% of the population. Drawn to people and transits that bridge the gap.
A major aspect formed when two planets are approximately 90 degrees apart in the zodiac. Squares represent friction, challenge, and dynamic tension that motivates action and growth through effort.

The moment when a retrograde planet appears to stop its backward motion and resumes forward (direct) movement. A planet stationing direct is considered especially powerful, as it concentrates energy at a single degree before moving forward again.

The moment when a planet appears to stop its forward motion and begins moving backward (retrograde). A planet stationing retrograde is considered intensely focused, marking a pivotal turning point in the themes governed by that planet.

A cluster of three or more planets concentrated in the same zodiac sign or house. A stellium intensely focuses energy in that area of life, making it a dominant theme in the chart and often the defining feature of a person's experience.

The correct way for each Type to make decisions and engage with life. Each Type has a unique Strategy: Manifestors inform, Generators and Manifesting Generators respond, Projectors wait for invitation, and Reflectors wait a lunar cycle.
The 2nd, 5th, 8th, and 11th houses, which follow the angular houses. Succedent houses are associated with stability, resources, and the consolidation of energy initiated by the angular houses.
The Signature theme of the Projector Type. When waiting for correct recognition and invitation before sharing guidance, genuine success follows.

The central luminary in astrology, representing core identity, ego, vitality, and life purpose. The Sun's sign placement is the most commonly known element of a birth chart and takes approximately one month to transit each zodiac sign.
The Signature theme of the Reflector Type. When waiting through a full lunar cycle and allowing life to unfold, they experience the delight of surprise.
One of six Perspective types (Color 1). Sees the world through security and self-preservation.

The fifteenth nakshatra in Vedic astrology, spanning 6 degrees 40 minutes to 20 degrees of Libra. Its ruling deity is Vayu (god of wind), and its planetary ruler is Rahu. It is associated with independence, flexibility, and the ability to adapt and thrive in changing conditions.

A technique for analyzing relationship compatibility by overlaying two natal charts and examining the aspects between them. Synastry reveals the dynamic interplay between two individuals, highlighting areas of harmony, attraction, and potential conflict.

The angular relationships formed between planets in one person's chart and planets in another person's chart, used to evaluate compatibility and relational dynamics. Key aspects include conjunctions, trines, squares, and oppositions between personal planets. Synastry is the most common technique for analyzing romantic, friendship, and business partnerships.

An aspect pattern formed when two planets in opposition both square a third planet, creating a T-shape in the chart. The focal planet at the apex of the T-Square receives concentrated tension and becomes a driving force for action and achievement.
One of six Cognition types (Tone 2). Processes information by sampling and discerning quality.
One of six Determination types (Color 2). The body selects food based on how it tastes, using the palate as the primary guide.
The second sign of the zodiac, spanning 30 to 60 degrees of the ecliptic. Taurus is a fixed earth sign ruled by Venus, associated with stability, sensuality, and material security.

A system of essential dignity that divides each zodiac sign into unequal segments, each ruled by a different planet. The terms or bounds provide a finer level of dignity assessment, with multiple systems (Egyptian, Ptolemaic) offering slightly different divisions.

A legendary mythical chart representing the birth of the world, used as a teaching tool in Hellenistic astrology. The Thema Mundi places Cancer on the Ascendant with the classical planets in their domiciles, illustrating the rationale behind sign rulerships, aspects, and the structure of the zodiac.
One of six Determination types (Color 3). Proper hydration is key to digestion, with liquid intake playing a central role.
The center of manifestation, communication, and expression. It is the hub through which all energy seeks to express itself, whether through speech or action.

A collective term for various planetary period systems used in Hellenistic and medieval astrology to determine which planets are most active during specific life periods. These include techniques such as decennials, zodiacal releasing, and annual profections. Time lord systems share a conceptual similarity with the Vedic dasha system.

A lunar day in the Vedic calendar, defined as the time it takes for the Moon to gain 12 degrees of separation from the Sun. There are 30 tithis in a complete lunar month (15 in the waxing half, 15 in the waning half). Tithis are a core element of the panchang and are used for muhurta (electional) timing.

The specific frequency within a Color that further refines how energy operates. There are six Tones in each Color, adding another layer of differentiation to the Line, Color, and Base structure of each activation.
One of six Cognition types (Tone 6). Processes information through physical contact and tactile, hands-on experience.
One of six Determination types (Color 4). The body is sensitive to temperature and texture of food.
The overall left or right orientation. Left Trajectory means strategic and focused. Right Trajectory means receptive and open.

The current planetary positions and their effect on the global neutrino field. Transits affect everyone and create a background frequency that can activate Gates and Channels temporarily, influencing collective themes.

A chart showing the current or future positions of planets compared to a natal chart. Transits are the primary predictive technique in modern astrology, revealing how ongoing planetary movements activate and influence natal placements over time.

The slow-moving outer planets Uranus, Neptune, and Pluto, which represent collective and generational forces. Because they spend many years in each sign, transpersonal planets shape the characteristics and challenges of entire generations rather than individuals.
Contains the Ego and Defense Circuits. Theme is support, resources, values, and community bonds. Operates through touch and the material world.

The 1st, 5th, and 9th houses in Vedic astrology, considered the most auspicious and dharmic positions in the chart. Trikona lords are natural benefics for any ascendant and their conjunction with kendra lords produces raja yoga. These houses relate to purpose (1st), creativity and merit (5th), and fortune and higher wisdom (9th).

A major aspect formed when two planets are approximately 120 degrees apart, typically connecting signs of the same element. Trines represent harmony, natural talent, and an easy flow of energy between the planets involved.
Three separate areas of definition with no connections between them. About 11% of the population. Need to move through different environments to experience bridging.

A system of essential dignity that assigns planetary rulers to each of the four elements (fire, earth, air, water). Triplicity rulers vary by sect, with different planets ruling an element by day and by night, adding nuance to planetary strength assessment.

The zodiac system based on the seasons, where 0 degrees Aries aligns with the vernal equinox. It is the standard framework used in Western astrology and does not account for the precession of the equinoxes.

A zodiac system that aligns sign boundaries with the actual astronomical constellations as defined by the International Astronomical Union (IAU). Unlike the standard sidereal zodiac (which uses equal 30-degree signs), true sidereal accounts for the unequal sizes of constellations. This means some signs span more or less than 30 degrees.

One of the five energy types in Human Design that determines how a person is designed to interact with the world. The five Types are Manifestor, Generator, Manifesting Generator, Projector, and Reflector.
A white center containing activated Gates but no complete channel. Takes in and amplifies energy from others, creating areas of conditioning and potential wisdom.

A planetary condition where a planet is within approximately 15 to 17 degrees of the Sun but outside the combust range. Under the beams is a milder debility than combustion, where the planet's significations are somewhat obscured but not entirely overwhelmed.

The 3rd, 6th, 10th, and 11th houses in Vedic astrology, known as houses of growth and improvement over time. Natural malefics (Sun, Mars, Saturn, Rahu, Ketu) perform well in upachaya houses because their aggressive energy is channeled into productive effort. Results associated with these houses tend to improve as the native ages.

The planet of revolution, innovation, sudden change, and individuality in astrology. Uranus is the modern ruler of Aquarius and takes approximately 84 years to orbit the Sun, spending about seven years in each sign.
Around age 38-42, the classic midlife crisis point. In Human Design, this is when pressure to awaken to authentic self peaks.

The twenty-first nakshatra in Vedic astrology, spanning 26 degrees 40 minutes of Sagittarius to 10 degrees of Capricorn. Its ruling deity is the Vishvadevas (universal gods), and its planetary ruler is the Sun. It is associated with final victory, lasting achievement, and unchallengeable authority.

The twenty-sixth nakshatra in Vedic astrology, spanning 3 degrees 20 minutes to 16 degrees 40 minutes of Pisces. Its ruling deity is Ahir Budhnya (serpent of the deep), and its planetary ruler is Saturn. It is associated with deep wisdom, spiritual depth, and the ability to control kundalini energy.

The twelfth nakshatra in Vedic astrology, spanning 26 degrees 40 minutes of Leo to 10 degrees of Virgo. Its ruling deity is Aryaman (god of patronage and friendship), and its planetary ruler is the Sun. It is associated with social contracts, marriage, and helpful generosity.

The Sanskrit term for retrograde motion in Vedic astrology, when a planet appears to move backward through the zodiac from Earth's perspective. A vakri planet is considered strong in Vedic tradition (unlike the Western view of weakened retrograde planets) because it is closest to Earth during retrograde. However, it may deliver its results in unexpected, internalized, or delayed ways.
One of six Environment types (Color 5). A low-lying, acoustically rich space where sound and vibration channel naturally.

A planet that occupies the same sign in both the birth chart (rashi) and the navamsa chart (D9) in Vedic astrology. Vargottama planets are considered exceptionally strong because their energy is consistent and reinforced across both charts. This condition is especially powerful for the Ascendant degree.

An advanced area of Human Design that examines the four Arrows at the top of the bodygraph. Variable determines how you are designed to take in nutrition (Determination), perceive the world (Cognition), be in the right place (Environment), and see correctly (Perspective).
Shorthand for the four Variable arrows: P (Personality) and D (Design) combined with R (Right) and L (Left). Examples: PRL DRL, PLL DRR.
The planet of love, beauty, values, and pleasure in astrology. Venus rules Taurus and Libra and represents how a person approaches relationships, aesthetics, and material comfort.

Aspects between Venus and Mars in synastry that are considered primary indicators of physical attraction and sexual chemistry between two people. Conjunctions, trines, and sextiles tend to produce harmonious desire, while squares and oppositions create intense but potentially volatile magnetism. These aspects are among the most immediately felt in relationship astrology.

The position of the Sun at the moment of the vernal (spring) equinox, which defines 0 degrees Aries in the Tropical zodiac. Due to precession, the vernal point slowly moves backward through the sidereal constellations over thousands of years.

A sensitive point in the western hemisphere of the birth chart, often called the point of fate. The Vertex is associated with fated encounters, significant relationships, and turning points that feel destined rather than chosen.

An asteroid representing devotion, sacred focus, and the inner flame in astrology. Vesta indicates where a person directs their deepest commitment and how they experience dedication, ritual, and self-sufficiency.
The Perspective derived from Personality Sun/Earth Color. How the mind is designed to see and interpret the world.

The most widely used planetary period system in Vedic astrology, based on a 120-year cycle divided among nine grahas. The sequence and starting point are determined by the Moon's nakshatra at birth. Each planet's period activates the themes of the houses it rules and occupies in the birth chart.
The sixth sign of the zodiac, spanning 150 to 180 degrees of the ecliptic. Virgo is a mutable earth sign ruled by Mercury, associated with analysis, service, and attention to detail.

The sixteenth nakshatra in Vedic astrology, spanning 20 degrees of Libra to 3 degrees 20 minutes of Scorpio. Its ruling deities are Indra and Agni, and its planetary ruler is Jupiter. It is associated with determination, single-pointed focus, and achieving goals through sustained effort.

A condition where a planet, most commonly the Moon, makes no more major aspects before leaving its current sign. A void of course Moon is traditionally considered unfavorable for initiating new projects, as actions taken during this period may not produce the intended results.

The specific period when the Moon makes no more major Ptolemaic aspects before changing signs. Actions initiated during a void of course Moon are traditionally considered unlikely to produce significant results, making it a better time for routine tasks and rest.
An individual's role or way within a group dynamic, describing their specific contribution within a Penta or larger structure.

The final lunar phase before the new moon when only a thin sliver of light remains visible. Also called the balsamic moon, this phase represents rest, surrender, healing, and preparation for the next cycle of renewal.

The lunar phase following the full moon when the illumination begins to decrease. Also called the disseminating phase, this period is associated with sharing knowledge, distributing what has been gained, and beginning the process of release.
One of six Perspective types (Color 4). Sees the world through what is lacking or needed.
One of the four classical elements in astrology, encompassing Cancer, Scorpio, and Pisces. Water signs are associated with emotion, intuition, sensitivity, and depth of feeling.

The lunar phase occurring after the new moon when a thin sliver of light first becomes visible on the Moon's right side. This phase represents emerging intention, gathering momentum, and the initial push to move forward with new plans.

The lunar phase between the first quarter and the full moon when more than half the Moon is illuminated and growing. This phase represents refinement, analysis, and fine-tuning as efforts build toward the culmination of the full moon.

The oldest known house system, originating in Hellenistic astrology, where each house corresponds to an entire zodiac sign. The sign containing the Ascendant becomes the first house, the next sign becomes the second house, and so on. It has seen a major resurgence in modern practice due to its simplicity and historical authenticity.

An aspect pattern formed when two planets sextile each other and both quincunx a third planet, creating a narrow triangle pointing to the apex planet. Known as the Finger of God, the yod indicates a fated quality or special mission connected to the apex planet.

An ayanamsa based on the calculations of Sri Yukteshwar Giri, the guru of Paramahansa Yogananda, as presented in his book The Holy Science. It incorporates a unique theory of cyclical yugas (ages) and a shorter precessional cycle. It differs significantly from the Lahiri ayanamsa and is used primarily by followers of the Kriya Yoga lineage.

A Hellenistic timing technique attributed to Vettius Valens that divides life into major and minor periods based on the Lot of Spirit or Lot of Fortune. Zodiacal releasing reveals peak periods of career activity, purpose, and significant life chapters through a cascading system of time lord periods.
